_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
_meta.purpose: Cross-language correctness proof for the Hybrid D JWE. JWE compact bytes are NOT fixed (random nonce per encrypt is mandatory — counter-based nonces are PROHIBITED). Instead this vector pins claim shape, header shape, round-trip semantics, kid dispatch, AAD behavior, and the 60s TTL window.
_meta.key_derivation_note: Verifier key = HKDF(INTERNAL_PRINCIPAL_SECRET, 'eex/internal-principal/v1', 32). See hkdf-parity.json for the IKM→OKM byte mapping.

header_constraints: alg MUST be 'dir' — anything else (RSA-OAEP, A128KW, etc.) is a hard reject at decode_protected_header.
enc MUST be 'A256GCM' — anything else (A128GCM, A256CBC, etc.) is a hard reject.
kid MUST be present. Rotation flow: v2 mints under kid='internal-v2', verifier carries {'internal-v1': old_key, 'internal-v2': new_key} during grace window.
Unknown kid → 401 with body {error: 'unknown_kid', kid: <received>}. NEVER attempt all keys; that defeats the rotation control.
jwe_shape.aad: ASCII bytes of the encoded protected header (segment 1 of the compact form). NOT the decoded JSON. This is the JWE compact serialization spec per RFC 7516 §5.1.

claims_shape.device_id: optional — present when the request originated from a device-bearer auth path; null/absent when from a cookie session
claims_shape.req_id: ULID — per-request id for tracing across services

ttl.enforcement: exp must be > now at verify time. NO nbf skew applied — internal principal is for a single in-flight request and must be fresh.

round_trip_vector.verify: encrypt(derived_key, kid='internal-v1', claims) → JWE compact string; decrypt(keys={internal-v1: derived_key}, token) → claims (exact field equality).

negative_checks.name: nonce-uniqueness
negative_checks.assertion: Two encrypts of the same claim set with the same key MUST produce different JWE compact strings. The iv segment must differ (random 12-byte nonce per encrypt via OsRng or equivalent CSPRNG). Counter-based or timestamp-based nonces are PROHIBITED — nonce reuse with the same key in AES-GCM is catastrophic (recovers the GHASH key).

negative_checks.name: expired-rejected
negative_checks.assertion: Decrypt succeeds (the JWE is valid bytes); the verifier's enforce_time_window(claims, now) returns Expired when now > exp.

negative_checks.name: wrong-iss-rejected
negative_checks.assertion: A token with iss != 'eex' decrypts to claims but the verifier MUST reject with UnsupportedIss (or equivalent error code) before granting the caller's identity.

negative_checks.name: tampered-aad-rejects
negative_checks.assertion: Modifying the protected header (segment 1) of a valid token MUST cause decrypt to fail with DecryptFailed. AAD binds the header to the ciphertext — header tampering invalidates the tag.

negative_checks.name: unknown-kid-rejects
negative_checks.assertion: decrypt(keys={'internal-v1': k1}, token_with_kid='internal-v99') MUST fail with UnknownKid. Verifier MUST NOT brute-force all keys in the map.
